title: Mora Saiyya (Yeno Mounam) Lyrics Maalai Nerathu Mayakkam Song Lyrics
stanzas:
Mora Saiyya (Yeno Mounam)
Singer(s): Vijay Prakash, Shakthisree Gopalan
Lyrics: Amrit, Vivek

Yeno mounam
indha raagam yen sogam
thaalamey puriyala
baavame unarala

kadandha kaalam
maraindhu pogum
vasandha kaalam
ini varumey.
ini varumey. (2)

vaa en pakkaam
thaa un muththam
ini nee en sondham
yaen thayakkam

Yeno mounam
indha raagam yen sogam
thaalamey puriyala
baavame unarala

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song?

The song expresses feelings of love and longing, capturing the essence of silence and emotional depth.

What is the context of the song in the movie?

In Maalai Nerathu Mayakkam, the song reflects the protagonist's emotional journey and connection with their love interest.

Who are the singers of this song?

The song features the talented voices of Shakthisree Gopalan and Vijay Prakash.

Who composed the music for this song?

The music for Mora Saiyya (Yeno Mounam) was composed by Amrit.

When was the song released?

The song was released on January 5, 2016.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In Mora Saiyya (Yeno Mounam), the soothing melody encapsulates the feelings of love and introspection experienced by the characters in Maalai Nerathu Mayakkam. The song's lyrics delve into the depths of silence and emotional turmoil, making it a poignant moment in the film. With the harmonious blend of Shakthisree Gopalan and Vijay Prakash's voices, it evokes a sense of nostalgia and longing. This duet not only enhances the narrative but also resonates with anyone who has experienced the complexities of love.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Mora Saiyya (Yeno Mounam) from the Maalai Nerathu Mayakkam is a mesmerizing duet that beautifully captures the essence of love and longing. With the enchanting voices of Shakthisree Gopalan and Vijay Prakash, this song weaves a soothing melody that resonates deeply with the heart. Experience the magic of Amrit's composition that elevates the emotional depth of the film.
stanzaMeanings: Why is there silence in this melody, my sorrow cannot be understood, can you feel it?
Why is there silence in this melody, my sorrow?
Oh my beloved, oh my beloved.
My joys, my sorrows, who knows the changes?
Will the moments we see, the times we experience, ever come again?
The time that has passed will fade away, will the spring season come again? It will come again.
Oh my beloved, oh my beloved.
Come to me, your kiss, now you are my own, why this hesitation?
Together as one, we will forget our troubles.
Don't hold back, this is not right, this relationship has no meaning, it is not true.
Oh my beloved, oh my beloved.
Why is there silence in this melody, my sorrow cannot be understood, can you feel it?
Why is there silence in this melody, my sorrow?
Oh my beloved, oh my beloved.
